channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: A Medicinal Chemistry Perspective on Anticonvulsant Drug Design: Targets, Mechanisms, and Spiro Compounds
item.description: This review examines anticonvulsant drug design from a medicinal chemistry perspective, focusing on established drug targets, their mechanisms of action, and the role of spiro compounds as a structural class in anticonvulsant development. The analysis integrates current understanding of molecular ta

item.title: Critical Copilot vulnerability allowed hackers to seal 2FA code from users
item.description: SearchLeak exploit shows why the industry's approach to LLM security fails over and over.

item.title: Verifiable audit trails for ai-enabled biological design tools
item.description: The authors propose a tamper-evident audit-trail framework using cryptographic signing to establish a trustworthy chain of custody that can be used with any artificial intelligence–enabled biological design tool.

item.title: Show hn: agentbridge – translate and govern calls between AI agent protocols
item.description: Neutral interop + governance layer for multi-agent systems. Any protocol in, any protocol out, with identity, budgets, and a tamper-evident audit trail. - shadowhunter-92/agentbridge

item.title: A generalizable hi-c foundation model for chromatin architecture, single-cell and multiomics analysis across species
item.description: HiCFoundation is a foundation model pretrained on a large corpus of Hi-C data for comprehensive 3D genome and epigenomics analysis.

item.title: 3d multi-omics tumour atlases: from technology to biology and clinical translation
item.description: In this Review, Liu et al. describe the established and emerging tools for the generation of comprehensive 3D tumour atlases, the analysis of which has the potential to uncover novel biomarkers for risk stratification, early detection, preventive intervention…

item.title: Canadian health it market push connected care agenda
item.description: Canadian health IT buyers are prioritizing interoperability, data sovereignty, AI governance, cybersecurity, and workflow ROI.

item.title: Data standardization key to health tech ecosystem, tefca's interoperability goals
item.description: Together, the CMS Health Tech Ecosystem initiative and TEFCA are creating a pathway from voluntary innovation to nationwide interoperability, but true success hinges on consistent data standards.

item.title: Article: governing AI in the cloud: a practical guide for architects
item.description: In this article, the author outlines a practical approach to AI governance in the cloud, covering discovery of shadow AI, data classification at creation, IAM-based enforcement, policy-as-code, and operational controls. The article shows how organizations can…

item.title: Real-time lending: why modern digital infrastructure is the future of credit
item.description: The lending industry is shifting to real-time infrastructure to meet borrower demands for speed and data security. APIs and modern platforms enable quick access to financial data, enhancing risk management and customer experiences.

item.title: What is document AI?
item.description: * Document AI’s value is bigger than faster processing. It turns messy, high-volume documents like contracts, invoices, claims and forms into structured data that downstream systems can actually use. * Generative AI makes document AI more adaptable, but not …

item.title: Ascentage pharma presents multiple clinical updates at eha2026 congress
item.description: Poster presentations from ongoing clinical trials of two core assets, olverembatinib and lisaftoclax, highlight global innovation capabilities in hematologic malignancie

item.title: Adiposity and cancer: epidemiology, mechanisms and future perspectives
item.description: This Review discusses epidemiological trends for adiposity and cancer and summarizes mechanisms linking obesity to cancer development.

item.title: Decoding common and rare noncoding variant effects across cellular and developmental contexts
item.description: This study contributes a resource of predicted effects of noncoding variants on chromatin accessibility and a method to identify noncoding variants with extreme regulatory effects, with application to disease variant discovery.

item.title: Plasma proteomic signatures of cellular aging predict human disease
item.description: The biological age of individual cell types can be evaluated using plasma proteomics, revealing diverse aging profiles across more than 40 cell types and links between the accelerated aging of specific cell types and disease.

item.title: Meta tapped a pentagon supplier to prototype face recognition for its glasses
item.description: Rank One, whose board includes a former CIA deputy director and a former FBI science chief, supplied face recognition to Meta for internal development of its smart glasses app.

item.title: The AI layoff wave is becoming a powder keg | techcrunch
item.description: What makes this combustible: at the very moment that tens of thousands of workers are being shown the door, a small cohort of AI insiders is becoming wealthy on a scale that's hard to comprehend.

item.title: FDA Approves New Indication for Tzield (teplizumab) for Certain Pediatric Patients with Recently Diagnosed Stage 3 Type 1 Diabetes
item.description: On June 12, 2026, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration granted accelerated approval to Tzield (teplizumab) for a new indication, to delay the decline of insulin production in pediatric patients ages 8 through 17 years who have been recently diagnosed with Stage 3 type 1 diabetes (T1D).

item.title: Why we still need {admiral} in an age of AI
item.description: There is a version of the AI-in-pharma story that goes like this: LLMs are trained on vast amounts of R code, so they can write ADaM programs on demand. Packages like admiral {admiral} become optional — a style preference rather than a requi... Continue readi…

item.title: As anthropic suspends access to new models, india debates its AI future | techcrunch
item.description: As Anthropic suspends access to new models, India debates its AI future | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: Meta reportedly moves to unwind $2b manus deal after beijing's demand | techcrunch
item.description: Meta starts dismantling its $2 billion Manus acquisition after Beijing ordered the deal reversed.

item.title: Trump tried to block state AI regulations, but some states are forging ahead
item.description: Six months after President Donald Trump warned states not to regulate artificial intelligence, they are increasingly doing just that

item.title: AI is code – and can't be prompted into being smarter
item.description: From Java tests to Shai-Hulud, bots keep proving they'll swallow anything you feed them

item.title: U.s. order cutting access to anthropic's AI models sparks criticism
item.description: The U.S. government's order for Anthropic to withdraw its most powerful artificial intelligence models has sparked a wave of criticism from both advocates and opponents of AI regulation. On Friday evening, the San Francisco-based company announced that the U.…

item.title: Just months after trump warned states not to regulate AI, republican and democratic lawmakers are doing it anyway
item.description: A mix of Republican- and Democratic-led states have passed such laws this year, including Colorado, Connecticut, Idaho, Iowa, Nebraska and Oregon.

item.title: No, everyone is not using AI for everything
item.description: People are consuming AI like they eat meat: some are embracing it, some are limiting their use of it, and some are avoiding it altogether.

item.title: Some congressional lawmakers have attempted to push back against AI data centers — but they haven’t gotten far
item.description: Lawmakers have introduced legislation to restrict and investigate data centers as more Americans have voiced their opposition to them.

item.title: Expansion of ai-enabled imaging in routine clinical practice: implications for preventive care
item.description: The opportunity is not to replicate SimonMed—but to selectively adopt its technological strengths within Canada’s system constraints. The post Expansion of AI-enabled imaging in routine clinical practice: Implications for preventive care appeared first on Dig…

item.title: China may have accessed mythos
item.description: According to a new report from Semafor, the White House's decision to impose export restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos was driven in part by fears that it had been accessed by a group linked to China. If the Chinese government actually had access to Mythos 5 …

item.title: As AI companies race to go public, who else is along for the ride? | techcrunch
item.description: As AI companies race to go public, who else is along for the ride? | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: Openai says it's engaging 'constructively' with state ags about concerns
item.description: The Wall Street Journal reported that a coalition of state attorneys general have opened an investigation into OpenAI.

item.title: Anthropic's safety warnings may have just backfired — the government has pulled the plug on its most powerful AI
item.description: Anthropic isn't hiding its frustration. "We disagree that the finding of a narrow potential jailbreak should be cause for recalling a commercial model deployed to hundreds of millions of people," the company wrote in a blog post.

item.title: Anthropic says it’s taking claude fable 5 offline to comply with US government order
item.description: “The government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or ‘jailbreaking’ Fable 5,” the company said in a blog post.

item.title: A better way to manage all your screenshots
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 132, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, happy soccer, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) This week, I've been preparing for a month …

item.title: Here’s how AI agents can protect ev chargers
item.description: An AI agent system proposed by researchers in Spain promises to prevent energy theft and damage to EV chargers, as well as the critical energy infrastructure that powers them.

item.title: Anthropic pre-ipo market falls after US directive forces model shutdown
item.description: Anthropic says it disabled Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after a US government export-control directive, while pre-IPO-linked trading weakened.

item.title: Evicore by evernorth rated #1 as prior authorization becomes a top health plan technology acquisition priority
item.description: eviCore by Evernorth Rated #1 as Prior Authorization Becomes a Top Health Plan Technology Acquisition Prioritymorningstar.com

item.title: Eli lilly’s AI data push meets new obesity rivalry from pfizer
item.description: Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has invested in Abridge, an AI-enabled clinical documentation company, marking a move into AI supported clinical research and real-world data capture. The partnership is aimed at improving trial enrollment and real-world evidence generati…

item.title: This week’s awesome tech stories from around the web (through june 13)
item.description: Every week, we scour the web for important, insightful, and fascinating stories in science and technology. The post This Week’s Awesome Tech Stories From Around the Web (Through June 13) appeared first on SingularityHub.

item.title: Amazon security research reportedly led to the white house’s anthropic fable ban
item.description: According to the Wall Street Journal, the export control directive that led to Anthropic cutting off access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 was triggered in part by cybersecurity research from Amazon and conversations between CEO Andy Jassy and the White House. Accor…

item.title: Amazon CEO reportedly raised anthropic model concerns before government crackdown | techcrunch
item.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy may have been the source of security concerns that led Anthropic to cut off worldwide access to two models on Friday.

item.title: Openai faces investigation from state attorneys general | techcrunch
item.description: It's not clear which states are involved, but they're asking about everything from OpenAI's ad policies to its handling of health data.

item.title: World cup AI predictor now lets users ask daft what-ifs
item.description: Spoiler: It doesn't end well for Team Register

item.title: Anthropic cuts off fable 5 and mythos 5 access following government order
item.description: On Friday evening, the government ordered Anthropic to block access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all foreign nations, both inside and outside the US, due to national security concerns. That order included employees of Anthropic. To meet those demands, the comp…

item.title: The fcc wants to kill burner phones
item.description: Plus: AI bug hunting fuels Microsoft’s biggest-ever Patch Tuesday, ShinyHunters ransomware gang exploits an Oracle zero-day, and more.

item.title: Kpmg pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations | techcrunch
item.description: KPMG pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: How to build a virtual cell and biology scaling laws
item.description: Markov Biosciences, a startup in San Francisco, is betting that virtual cells will soon have their GPT moment.

item.title: Towards AI-augmented decision making in psychiatry
item.description: Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 12 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01256-2 Psychiatric disorders are heterogeneous, and care depends on interpreting unstructured longitudinal narratives, creating variability that hinders standardization. A study now shows that a psychiatry-specific

item.title: Atomic-level protein–ligand recognition with pbcnet2.0 for probe discovery
item.description: Protein–ligand binding affinity prediction is important for the development and optimization of molecular probes. PBCNet2.0 predicts relative affinity changes induced by ligand modifications (for example, functional groups and stereochemistry) and shows an em…

item.title: Stocks to watch today, 12 june: dabur india, vedanta, gng electronics, infosys, hexagon nutrition, irctc, genxai analytics, state bank of india (sbi), lenskart solutions, sagility
item.description: Stocks To Watch Today, 12 June: Dabur India, Vedanta, GNG Electronics, Infosys, Hexagon Nutrition, IRCTC, GenXAI Analytics, State Bank of India (SBI), Lenskart Solutions, Sagilitysundayguardianlive.com
